name: Sauteed Chicken Paprika
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, mix together matzo meal, paprika, dry mustard, salt (if desired), and pepper. Coat the chicken evenly with this mixture, reserving any leftover for later.
2. Heat 3 teaspoons of oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat until hot. Add the chicken; cook for 1 to 2 minutes on each side or until lightly browned. Remove the chicken from the skillet and keep warm.
3. In the same skillet, heat the remaining 1 teaspoon of oil. Add the onion and garlic; cook and stir until the onion is tender. Add the broth, wine, and reserved matzo mixture; mix well. Return the chicken to the skillet. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 7-9 minutes or until the chicken is fork tender and juices are clear. Stir in the tomato and continue cooking for an additional 2-3 minutes. Garnish with parsley.
